series: DM1 (CFS Divisia M1, level index, base 100 @ 1967-01)
divisia_dm1.csv.source: Center for Financial Stability, AMFM program (Barnett Divisia), Narrow worksheet, user-supplied workbook
divisia_dm1.csv.note: user-cost weighted; continuous across the May-2020 M1 redefinition

CURRSL.csv.source: FRED (Board of Governors, H.6), monthly seasonally-adjusted level

DEMDEPSL.csv.source: FRED (Board of Governors, H.6), monthly seasonally-adjusted level

OCDSL.csv.source: FRED (Board of Governors, H.6), monthly seasonally-adjusted level
OCDSL.csv.note: composition tier component (active): other-checkable deposits. DISCONTINUED after 2020-04 (H.6 Feb-2021 change stopped separate OCD reporting). Do NOT substitute MDLM, which folds savings into the transactional tier and defeats the test. The composition tier ends here by construction; Divisia carries 2021 onward.

constructions.Mt_composition: CURRSL + DEMDEPSL + OCDSL (currency + demand + other-checkable), savings held OUT. Built by src/build_mt.py:composition_granular; horserace in src/run_composition_horserace.py. Window 1959-01..2020-04.

payments_volumes (registered, not yet pu.ach_value_bil: Nacha, ACH Network Volume and Value statistics (annual), nacha.org
payments_volumes (registered, not yet pu.rtp_value_bil: The Clearing House, RTP network statistics (annual)
payments_volumes (registered, not yet pu.fedwire_nonfin_value_bil: FRB Financial Services, Fedwire Funds Service annual statistics, frbservices.org; non-financial exclusion rule per build_mt_paymentflow.py header
payments_volumes (registered, not yet pu.status: schema registered in build_mt_paymentflow.py; awaiting data pull

ach_value_bil.source: Nacha, ACH Network annual volume/value releases (network figures, excl. on-us)

values.2025: $93.00T -- nacha.org, 'ACH Network Volume and Value Statistics' + Jan 29 2026 release
values.2024: $86.2T -- Nacha Jan 30 2025 release (also 93.00/1.079 from the 2025 release's +7.9%)
values.2014: $40.0T -- derived exactly: 2015 release states 2015 value +4% over 2014 (41.6/1.04)
values.2013: $38.69T -- derived exactly: Nacha-sourced 2013-22 value gain of 98.25% (76.7/1.9825)

check_value_bil.source: Federal Reserve Payments Study (FRPS) triennial benchmarks; geometric interpolation between benchmarks

benchmarks.2015: $29.19T -- back-cast from revised 2018 at the FRPS-revised -2.8%/yr value trend (26.8/0.972^3)

check_value_bil.notes: 2013-2014 back-cast at -2.8%/yr; 2016-2017 and 2019-2020 geometric interpolation; 2022-2025 held flat at the 2021 benchmark (FRPS shows check VALUE roughly stable, +0.6%/yr 2018-2021, as counts fall and average value rises). Flat-hold is an assumption and is flagged in PAYMENTFLOW_RESULTS.md.

rtp_value_bil.source: The Clearing House RTP network releases

values.2023: $127B -- derived: TCH Jan 8 2025 release states 2024 value +94% over 2023 (246/1.94)
values.2025: $1,300B -- TCH Dec 4 2025 release: 'surpassed $1.3 trillion for 2025' through November; conservative floor for the full year

rtp_value_bil.notes: 2017-2022 left empty: cumulative network total since 2017 inception is ~$3T, so pre-2023 annual values are immaterial (<0.3% of P).

fedwire_nonfin_value_bil.source: INTENTIONALLY EMPTY -- Fedwire Funds gross value (frbservices.org annual statistics, e.g. $1,148.3T in 2025, average transfer $5.28M) is dominated by financial-circuit settlement (interbank funding, securities/FX legs). FRPS DFIPS 2021 corroborates: commercial wires $1,441T with interbank about two-thirds by number. No public non-financial decomposition exists; including gross wires would contaminate the goods-transactional measure with exactly the asset-circuit flows the M^T concept excludes. The exclusion and its direction are documented in PAYMENTFLOW_RESULTS.md.

payments_quarterly.csv (pulled 2026-07-0.source: Nacha quarterly ACH Network releases, 2021Q1-2025Q4; per-row basis column records whether each value is stated verbatim in a Nacha release, derived from a stated Nacha YoY percentage, or the annual-consistent residual quarter
payments_quarterly.csv (pulled 2026-07-0.integrity: each year sums exactly to the confirmed Nacha annual total (72.6/76.7/80.1/86.2/93.0 )

turnover_benchmark (results section D).source: Wheat, C. & Eckerd, G. (2023). Household Cash Buffer Management from the Great Recession through COVID-19. JPMorgan Chase Institute.
turnover_benchmark (results section D).figures_used: pre-pandemic (2009-2020) median cash buffers by income quartile: 13/16/19/26 days (Figure 1); buffer = liquid balances / trailing-12m median checking outflows ex transfers
